birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The tawny-chested flycatcher is now known only from northeastern Costa Rica, on the Caribbean slope from Alajuela Province south to central Limón Province. There are historical records from near Lake Nicaragua and its outflow the San Juan River, much of which forms the border with Costa Rica. The species inhabits humid mature secondary forest in the upper tropical and subtropical zones
